name: Broiled Tuna
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp + 1 tsp margarine, reduced calorie style
4 Tuna (sea bass or salmon), fillets - 3 to 4 oz each
recipeInstructions:
Suggested wine: Chardonnay
1. Spray broiler pan with nonstick cooking spray and preheat the broiler.
2. In a small saucepan, combine margarine, lemon juice, and olive oil; heat until the margarine melts. Add shallots and parsley; cook for 2 minutes. Sprinkle tuna with salt and pepper. Place one side of tuna into the shallot mixture, coating it evenly, on the prepared pan.
3. Broil tuna 4 inches from the heat source for 2-3 minutes. Turn over the tuna and spread the remaining shallot mixture evenly on top. Continue broiling for an additional 5 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
